---
title: "Module 2 Assignment: Spark SQL and DataFrames"
author:
  - name: Holly Morehouse
    affiliations:
      - id: bu
        name: Boston University
        city: Boston
        state: MA
number-sections: true
date: '2024-11-21'
format:
  html:
    theme: cerulean
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
  docx:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    number-sections: true
    syntax-highlighting: github
date-modified: today
date-format: long
execute:
  echo: false
  eval: true
  freeze: auto
---


## System Information {.unnumbered}

```{python}
# | eval: true
# | echo: true
import platform
import psutil

print("Operating System:", platform.system(), platform.release())
print("Python Version:", platform.python_version())
print("Machine:", platform.machine())
print("Processor:", platform.processor())

mem = psutil.virtual_memory()
print("Total Memory (MB):", round(mem.total / (1024**2), 2))
print("Available Memory (MB):", round(mem.available / (1024**2), 2))
```


## Start your assignment here

GitHub Repo: https://github.com/AD688-SP26/a02-sp26-hjmorehouse89/tree/main

## Test Load
```{python}
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession

# Start a Spark session
spark = SparkSession.builder.config("spark.driver.host", "localhost").appName("JobPostingsAnalysis").getOrCreate()
spark.catalog.clearCache()

# Load the CSV file into a Spark DataFrame
df = spark.read.option("header", "true").option("inferSchema", "true").option(
    "multiLine", "true").option("escape", "\"").csv("./data/lightcast_job_postings.csv")

# Register the DataFrame as a temporary SQL view
df.createOrReplaceTempView("jobs")

# Show Schema and Sample Data
print("---This is Diagnostic check, No need to print it in the final doc---")

# comment the lines below when rendering the submission
df.printSchema()
df.show(5)
```

## Jobs Test Table

```{python}
spark.sql("SELECT ID AS job_id, title_raw AS job_title, body AS job_description FROM jobs LIMIT 5").show(truncate=False)
from pyspark.sql import SparkSession
from pyspark.sql.functions import col, monotonically_increasing_id

#initialize Spark Session
spark = SparkSession.builder.appName("JobPostingsDB").getOrCreate()

#Load the Full Dataset
df = spark.read.option("header", "true").option("multiLine","true").option("escape","\"").csv("./data/lightcast_job_postings.csv")

df.show(5)

```

## Companies

```{python}

companies_df = df.select(
  col("ID"),
  col("company"),
  col("company_name"),
  col("company_raw"),
  col("company_is_staffing")
).distinct().withColumn("company_id", monotonically_increasing_id())
companies_df.show(5)
companies = companies_df.toPandas()
companies.drop(columns=["company"], inplace=True)
companies.rename(columns={"company_is_staffing": "is_staffing"}, inplace=True)
companies.to_csv("./output/companies.csv", index=False)
companies.head()
```


## Industries

```{python}
from pyspark.sql.functions import col, monotonically_increasing_id

industries_df = df.select(
    col("naics_2022_6").alias("industry_code"),
    col("naics_2022_6_name"),
    col("soc_5").alias("soc_code"),
    col("soc_5_name").alias("soc_name"),
    col("lot_specialized_occupation_name").alias("specialized_occupation"),
    col("lot_occupation_group").alias("occupation_group")
).distinct().withColumn("industry_id", monotonically_increasing_id())

# Rearrange columns to have industry_id first
industries_df = industries_df.select(
    "industry_id",
    "industry_code",
    "naics_2022_6_name",
    "soc_code",
    "soc_name",
    "specialized_occupation",
    "occupation_group"
)

industries_df.show(5, truncate=False)
```

## Locations

```{python}

locations_df = df.select(
  col("location").alias("lat-long"),
  col("city_name"),
  col("state_name"),
  col("county_name"),
  col("msa"),
  col("msa_name")
).distinct().withColumn("location_id", monotonically_increasing_id())
locations_df.show(5,truncate=False)

```

```{python}
from pyspark.sql.functions import from_json, col
from pyspark.sql.types import StructType, StructField, DoubleType

location_schema = StructType([
  StructField("lat", DoubleType(), True),
  StructField("lon", DoubleType(), True)
])

locations_df2 = locations_df \
    .withColumn("location_struct", from_json(col("lat-long"),location_schema)) \
        .withColumn("latitude", col("location_struct.lat")) \
            .withColumn("longitude", col("location_struct.lon")) \
                .drop("location_struct")

locations_df2.show(5, truncate=False)

locations_clean = locations_df2.select(
  col("location_id"),
  col("latitude"),
  col("longitude"),
  col("city_name"),
  col("county_name"),
  col("state_name"),
  col("msa").alias("msa_id"),
  col("msa_name")
)

locations_clean.show(5, truncate=False)

```

## Job Postings

```{python}
job_postings_df = df.select(
  col("id").alias("job_id"),
  col("title_clean"),
  col("body"),
  col("company"),
  col("employment_type_name"),
  col("remote_type_name"),
  col("min_years_experience"),
  col("max_years_experience"),
  col("salary"),
  col("salary_from"),
  col("salary_to"),
  col("location"),
  col("naics_2022_6").alias("industry_code"),
  col("posted"),
  col("expired"),
  col("duration")
)

#job_postings_df.show(5)

job_postings_df = job_postings_df.join(companies_df.select("company","company_id"),
                                        on="company", how="left")
job_postings_df = job_postings_df.join(locations_df.select("lat-long","location_id"),
                                        job_postings_df.location == locations_df["lat-long"],
                                        how="left").drop("location")
job_postings_df = job_postings_df.join(industries_df.select("industry_code","industry_id"),
                                        job_postings_df.industry_code == industries_df.industry_code, 
                                        how="left").drop("industry_code")

job_postings_df = job_postings_df.drop("company", "lat-long")

job_postings_df.show(5)

```

## Register Tables in Spark

```{python}
job_postings_df.createOrReplaceTempView("job_postings")
companies_df.createOrReplaceTempView("companies")
industries_df.createOrReplaceTempView("industries")
locations_df.createOrReplaceTempView("locations")
```

```{python}
distinct_specialized_occupations = spark.sql("SELECT DISTINCT specialized_occupation FROM industries")
distinct_specialized_occupations.show(truncate=False)
```

## Query 1

```{python}
from pyspark.sql import functions as F
#from pyspark.sql import SparkSession
#spark = SparkSession.builder.appName("JobPostingsDB").getOrCreate()


tech_salary_df = spark.sql("""
  SELECT i.specialized_occupation, i.naics_2022_6_name AS industry_name, PERCENTILE_APPROX(j.salary, 0.5) AS median_salary
  FROM job_postings j
  JOIN industries i ON j.industry_id = i.industry_id
  WHERE j.salary IS NOT NULL
      AND j.salary > 0
      AND i.industry_code = '518210'
  GROUP BY i.specialized_occupation, industry_name 
  ORDER BY median_salary DESC
  """)

tech_salary_df.show()
```


## Query 1 Plotly Plot
```{python}
import plotly.express as px
fig = px.bar(tech_salary_df, x='median_salary', y='specialized_occupation',color='industry_name', width=800)
fig.update_layout(legend=dict(
  orientation="h",
  yanchor="bottom",
  y=1.02,
  xanchor="right",
  x=1
))
fig.show()

```

## Query 2

```{python}
from pyspark.sql import functions as F
#from pyspark.sql import SparkSession
#spark = SparkSession.builder.appName("JobPostingsDB").getOrCreate()


remote_jobs_df = spark.sql("""
  SELECT COUNT(j.remote_type_name) AS remote_count, l.state_name, c.company_name 
  FROM job_postings j
  JOIN companies c ON j.company_id = c.company_id
  JOIN locations l ON j.location_id = l.location_id
  WHERE l.state_name IS NOT NULL
      AND l.state_name = 'California'
      AND j.remote_type_name = 'Remote'
      AND c.company_name <> 'Unclassified'
  GROUP BY c.company_name, l.state_name
  ORDER BY remote_count DESC
  LIMIT 5
  """)

remote_jobs_df.show()
```

## Query 2 Plot

```{python}
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

remote_jobs_pd = remote_jobs_df.toPandas()

if remote_jobs_pd.empty:
  print("Uh Oh, your Table is empty")
else:
  sns.set_theme(style="whitegrid")
  plt.figure(figsize=(12,6))

  ax = sns.barplot(
    data=remote_jobs_pd,
    x= "company_name",
    y= "remote_count"
  )
  
  ax.set_xlabel("Company Name")
  ax.set_ylabel("Remote Jobs")
  ax.set_title("Remote Jobs in California")

  plt.savefig("./figures/Remote_Jobs_Plot.png", dpi=300, bbox_inches="tight")

  plt.show()

```
